copyright: © 2026 NexVigilant, LLC. Harm Taxonomy from Theory of Vigilance §9 by Matthew A. Campion, PharmD.
title: Harm Taxonomy — 8-Type Classification from Conservation Law Violations
description: Systematic taxonomy of 8 harm types (A-H) derived from 2³ combinatorics of perturbation multiplicity × temporal profile × response determinism. Maps each type to conservation laws violated, manifestation hierarchy levels, axiom connections, and cross-domain applications. Pure Rust via nexcore-harm-taxonomy.

tools.name: classify
tools.description: Classify a harm event by three binary attributes: multiplicity (single/multiple), temporal profile (acute/chronic), and response determinism (deterministic/stochastic). Returns best-matching harm type (A-H) with confidence, conservation laws violated, and manifestation levels.

tools.name: catalog
tools.description: Return the complete catalog of all 8 harm types (A-H) with definitions, conservation laws violated, manifestation levels, and examples. Use to understand the full taxonomy before classifying.

tools.name: axiom-connection
tools.description: Get the conservation law axiom connection for a specific harm type. Returns primary and secondary axioms violated, mechanism of violation, and intervention strategy.

parameters.name: harm_type
parameters.description: Harm type letter: A, B, C, D, E, F, G, or H

tools.name: manifestation-levels
tools.description: Get the hierarchy manifestation levels for a harm type. Returns the range of levels (1-8: Molecular to Population) where this harm type manifests and the derivation basis.

parameters.name: harm_type
parameters.description: Harm type letter: A through H

tools.name: combinations
tools.description: List common harm type combinations that co-occur in clinical practice. Returns multi-type patterns with their frequency and clinical significance.

tools.name: verify-exhaustiveness
tools.description: Verify that the 8-type taxonomy is exhaustive — that all 2³=8 combinations of the three binary characteristics are covered. Returns the proof with any gaps found.
